title: incident-bot: lightweight diagnostic toolbelt + ephemeral debug sessions for responders
preview: Motivation: on-call responders repeatedly run the same set of manual diagnostics when an alert fires (span tailing, recent-error log windows, lightweight heap/alloc snapshots, and quick-curl checks). This PR introduces a compact 'diagnostic toolbelt' exposed by incident-bot and an ephemeral debug session mode that l...
text: Motivation: on-call responders repeatedly run the same set of manual diagnostics when an alert fires (span tailing, recent-error log windows, lightweight heap/alloc snapshots, and quick-curl checks). This PR introduces a compact 'diagnostic toolbelt' exposed by incident-bot and an ephemeral debug session mode that lets responders run short-lived, auditable diagnostics without escalation to infra. Goals: reduce MTTD by providing one-click diagnostics, keep operations safe via TTLs and RBAC checks, and ensure cost caps through soft quotas and telemetry. What this change does: 1) Adds a new /v1/incidents/{id}/toolbelt API surface that aggregates a configurable set of diagnostics (logs tail, span tail, recent-errors, quick-rerun of failing healthcheck endpoints, and a heap-lite snapshot request). 2) Implements ephemeral debug sessions: a generated session token with a 15-minute TTL that g...

title: adaptive-signal-attenuator-and-stepwise-playbook-generator
preview: Motivation: On-call noise and low-signal alerts keep interrupting responders and dilute attention away from high-impact incidents. This PR introduces two complementary features: (1) an adaptive signal attenuator that temporarily mutes or down-prioritizes low-signal alert bursts based on multivariate heuristics (enti...
text: Motivation: On-call noise and low-signal alerts keep interrupting responders and dilute attention away from high-impact incidents. This PR introduces two complementary features: (1) an adaptive signal attenuator that temporarily mutes or down-prioritizes low-signal alert bursts based on multivariate heuristics (entity cardinality, recent noise history, error-rate delta), and (2) a stepwise playbook generator that converts canonical runbook templates into prioritized, step-by-step remediation checklists with embedded conditional telemetry checks. Both are exposed via the incident-bot core and a new CLI surface (oncallctl) and are designed for safe gradual rollout with canary attenuation and an explicit override flow.

title: Real-time Runbook Compiler and Telemetry Stitcher
preview: Context and motivation: On-call responders frequently have to assemble fragmented signals (alerts, traces, logs, SLO deltas) into an actionable runbook under time pressure. This PR introduces a Runbook Compiler service that composes a compact, prioritized runbook from a set of incident signals and a Telemetry Stitch...
text:
Context and motivation: On-call responders frequently have to assemble fragmented signals (alerts, traces, logs, SLO deltas) into an actionable runbook under time pressure. This PR introduces a Runbook Compiler service that composes a compact, prioritized runbook from a set of incident signals and a Telemetry Stitcher that pulls and correlates relevant telemetry slices across services. The goal is to reduce TTR and make playbook suggestions reproducible and auditable.

What this PR does (summary):
- Add runbook-compiler microservice (go) that accepts an incident payload and returns a prioritized runbook (steps + rationale + safety gates).
- Add telemetry-stitcher worker (python) that builds correlated telemetry slices (spans, top traces, key logs, metric cutouts) and applies automatic sanitization rules to strip PII/keys.
- Add ephemeral-debug-proxy (node) that can safely replay a lim...

title: runbook-telemetry-heuristics-and-blindspot-prober
preview: Problem: our runbook suggestion flow surface-level checks (restarts, scaling, config flags) but frequently misses gaps in telemetry — 'blindspots' where a missing or mis-tagged metric/tracing span causes responders to chase symptoms. This PR adds a lightweight heuristics engine that analyzes incoming incident contex...
text:
Problem: our runbook suggestion flow surface-level checks (restarts, scaling, config flags) but frequently misses gaps in telemetry — 'blindspots' where a missing or mis-tagged metric/tracing span causes responders to chase symptoms. This PR adds a lightweight heuristics engine that analyzes incoming incident context + historical telemetry expectations and surfaces likely blindspots, and a safe 'blindspot prober' that can run read-only checks against telemetry pipelines to validate assumptions.

What this does (high level):
- Adds a telemetry heuristics module to incident-bot that computes a blindspot_score for an incident based on absent expected signals (metrics/traces/log markers) and signal-quality indicators (sample rates, tag variance, TTL gaps).
- Introduces a blindspot prober that runs ephemeral, read-only queries (metrics histogram checks, span-presence checks, and tag-resolu...

title: Contextual Hypothesis Writer and Sandboxed Fix Prover
preview:
Context and motivation:
This PR adds two coordinated features to the incident-bot suite: (1) a Contextual Hypothesis Writer that synthesizes prioritized remediation hypotheses from alert telemetry and runbook fragments, and (2) a Sandboxed Fix Prover that attempts safe, read-only or reversible dry-run fixes in ephem...
text:
Context and motivation:
This PR adds two coordinated features to the incident-bot suite: (1) a Contextual Hypothesis Writer that synthesizes prioritized remediation hypotheses from alert telemetry and runbook fragments, and (2) a Sandboxed Fix Prover that attempts safe, read-only or reversible dry-run fixes in ephemeral sandboxes to validate hypotheses before recommending operator action. The goal is to reduce mean time to mitigation for operational incidents while keeping all experiments isolated and auditable.

High-level design and behavior:
- Hypothesis Writer consumes the incident snapshot (logs, traces, metrics, recent deploys, route maps) and runbook snippets to propose 2-5 ranked hypotheses with rationale and confidence score. It attaches minimal reproduction hints and a short sequence of commands or API calls required to validate the hypothesis.
- Fix Prover executes validati...

title: Sampled probe choreographer and on-call handoff scorecard
preview: Motivation: Reduce noisy probes and pager fatigue while improving first-responder context during handoffs by sampling probes intelligently and producing a compact handoff scorecard. This PR implements a sampled probe choreographer that schedules a small, representative set of lightweight probes per on-call window, t...
text: Motivation: Reduce noisy probes and pager fatigue while improving first-responder context during handoffs by sampling probes intelligently and producing a compact handoff scorecard. This PR implements a sampled probe choreographer that schedules a small, representative set of lightweight probes per on-call window, tracks probe provenance, and emits a one-line handoff scorecard summarizing signal health and probe coverage. Changes include: 1) a new choreographer component (choreographer/sampled_scheduler.py) that picks probes deterministically using a weighted reservoir sampler tied to on-call rotation ids; 2) probe metadata propagation to the incident snapshot (snapshot/attachments.md -> snapshot metadata additions) so responders can see sample reason and TTL; 3) a handoff scorecard generator (handoff/scorecard.py) that produces a compact human-friendly summary and machine-readable ta...

title: Enrich on-call diagnostics with automated runbook suggestions and triage helpers
preview: Context: On-call teams often waste critical minutes assembling the same diagnostic surface (pod logs, recent deploys, graph snapshots, SLO deltas) before they can follow runbooks. This PR adds an automated diagnostics gatherer plus a lightweight runbook-suggestion engine to incident-bot so on-call responders get an ...
text: Context: On-call teams often waste critical minutes assembling the same diagnostic surface (pod logs, recent deploys, graph snapshots, SLO deltas) before they can follow runbooks. This PR adds an automated diagnostics gatherer plus a lightweight runbook-suggestion engine to incident-bot so on-call responders get an actionable triage checklist and templated next steps in Slack/PagerDuty threads. Motivation: reduce mean time to acknowledge (MTTA) and mean time to resolution (MTTR) by surfacing high-value signals automatically and recommending runbook steps tailored to the observed signals. Summary of changes: 1) diagnostics worker: new background worker that gathers a bounded set of artifacts (pod logs tails, pod event summaries, recent deploy metadata, SLO deltas from slo-toolkit, topology hints) with configurable TTL and size limits; 2) suggestion engine: rule-based scorer + template ...

title: Post rollout auto-rollback events to Slack (concise notification + links)
preview: Summary: incident-bot now posts a Slack notification when the rollout controller triggers an automatic safety action due to an SLO breach (auto_rollback, auto_pause, or rollback_suppressed). The message is intentionally short, includes the rollout_id and scope, and provides deep links to (1) the rollout in Console, ...
text: Summary: incident-bot now posts a Slack notification when the rollout controller triggers an automatic safety action due to an SLO breach (auto_rollback, auto_pause, or rollback_suppressed). The message is intentionally short, includes the rollout_id and scope, and provides deep links to (1) the rollout in Console, (2) the rollout SLO dashboard, and (3) the audit event details. Motivation: during the 2026-02 simulations and recent customer escalations, oncall often learned about auto-mitigations only after secondary alerts or customer reports; this closes the loop and reduces time-to-context while keeping noise bounded. Event source: consumes the new structured audit event emitted to the event stream (redwood PR-28435) with event_type=rollout.auto_action.*. The handler maps event subtypes to Slack severity and routing without paging by default (notify-only) to avoid paging storms. Mes...

title: Add bridged on-call context and automated escalation templates
preview: Motivation: on-call responders frequently need full incident context when incidents are bridged across multiple chat channels and paging systems; manual copy/paste is error-prone and slows TTR. This PR introduces a compact, serializable "bridged context" snapshot that travels with the incident across channels and an...
text: Motivation: on-call responders frequently need full incident context when incidents are bridged across multiple chat channels and paging systems; manual copy/paste is error-prone and slows TTR. This PR introduces a compact, serializable "bridged context" snapshot that travels with the incident across channels and an opinionated library of parameterized escalation templates that the bot can surface or send automatically. Changes include: 1) context capture: snapshot of current runbook pointer, top-3 alerts, recent error rates, and active mitigations; 2) context propagation: add signed metadata token to bridged messages so downstream responders receive identical context; 3) template library: YAML-driven templates with conditional rendering, throttling and dedupe keys; 4) escalation rules: configurable selectors that map alert signals + SLO breach state to template choices and escalation...

title: oncall handoff checksum and telemetry synthesizer for compact context transfer
preview: Motivation: handoffs during high-pressure incidents frequently lost critical context (partial traces, clipped logs, ad-hoc snippets). This PR adds two complementary features to reduce costly context gaps: 1) a deterministic handoff checksum attached to every incident-bot handoff message that verifies payload fidelit...
text: Motivation: handoffs during high-pressure incidents frequently lost critical context (partial traces, clipped logs, ad-hoc snippets). This PR adds two complementary features to reduce costly context gaps: 1) a deterministic handoff checksum attached to every incident-bot handoff message that verifies payload fidelity across tools; 2) a telemetry synthesizer service that constructs a compact, privacy-safe summary of missing spans/events when downstream telemetry is sparse. The checksum is a canonical JSON serialization + SHA256 digest with stable ordering and configurable salt, surfaced as 'handoff_checksum' in handoff payloads and displayed in the oncall UI; operators can use it to assert the handoff message received by downstream runbooks/Slack threads matches the sent content. The telemetry synthesizer runs as an opt-in stage during handoff: it inspects available traces/logs, infers...

title: evidence-pack-assembler-and-runbook-heuristics
preview: Summary: This change adds an "evidence pack" assembler to the incident-bot pipeline and a set of runbook heuristics to automatically curate the most relevant diagnostics for an alert. The assembler collects contextual artifacts (metrics snapshots, recent logs, trace spans, KV cache summaries, and SLO delta snapshots...
text:
Summary: This change adds an "evidence pack" assembler to the incident-bot pipeline and a set of runbook heuristics to automatically curate the most relevant diagnostics for an alert. The assembler collects contextual artifacts (metrics snapshots, recent logs, trace spans, KV cache summaries, and SLO delta snapshots), ranks them using lightweight heuristics, prunes/pivots sensitive fields, and generates a compressed evidence bundle that can be attached to runbooks and pager notifications.

Motivation and context: On long-running incidents engineers spend a lot of time pulling the same set of data from multiple systems for triage. This PR is targeted at reducing mean time to acknowledgement and the handoff friction by producing a focused, size-bounded artifact that the oncall and responder can consume in the first 10 minutes. It also standardizes what we include for downstream postmort...

title: Proactive mitigations simulation harness and playbook safety gates
preview: Motivation: On several recent incidents automated mitigations triggered by the incident-bot caused partial service disruption because there was no pre-run simulation of stateful actions and limited safety gating. This PR introduces a lightweight simulation harness that executes mitigation steps against a sandboxed s...
text:
Motivation: On several recent incidents automated mitigations triggered by the incident-bot caused partial service disruption because there was no pre-run simulation of stateful actions and limited safety gating. This PR introduces a lightweight simulation harness that executes mitigation steps against a sandboxed state model and a set of declarative safety gates that must pass before the bot can apply real-world actions.

What this PR does (single-string description): Adds a mitigation simulation harness, a safety-gate DSL for playbooks, a test suite with representative incident scenarios, and small plumbing to record simulation traces in S3 for post-run analysis. Includes documentation, a canary rollout path, CLI helpers (incubate mode) and a feature flag to opt-in per-team.

Checklist: 
- [x] Simulation harness core runtime (deterministic, time-boxed) 
- [x] Safety-gate DSL and eva...

title: quickcheck: add lightweight oncall quick-check CLI and compact diagnostic packer
preview: Implements a small, dependency-light quick-check CLI and a compact diagnostic packer that incident responders can run from the incident channel or a runbook step. Motivation: many oncall incidents are prolonged by manual ad-hoc data collection and noisy attachments. This PR provides a safe, opinionated tool that cap...
text: Implements a small, dependency-light quick-check CLI and a compact diagnostic packer that incident responders can run from the incident channel or a runbook step. Motivation: many oncall incidents are prolonged by manual ad-hoc data collection and noisy attachments. This PR provides a safe, opinionated tool that captures a bounded set of artifacts (sanitized logs, pod status, recent traces, KV cache hints) and emits a single compressed artifact suitable for attaching to an incident thread or uploading to the internal diagnostics bucket. Key properties: fast (<= 15s typical), bounded output size (configurable default 8MB), privacy-first sanitization hooks, dry-run mode, and automatic S3-compatible upload with short-lived presigned URLs. Includes a new slash-command integration for incident-bot (/quickcheck) to trigger the CLI remotely via action runners and a small Webhook consumer use...

title: handoff: privacy filter and attachment rate-limit proxy
preview: Add a configurable PII redaction pipeline to handoff notes and an ephemeral attachment proxy with per-incident and per-user rate limits. Motivation: customers reported accidental PII leakage in handoff notes and large attachments causing spiky egress and long-term retention burdens. This PR implements a layered appr...
text: Add a configurable PII redaction pipeline to handoff notes and an ephemeral attachment proxy with per-incident and per-user rate limits. Motivation: customers reported accidental PII leakage in handoff notes and large attachments causing spiky egress and long-term retention burdens. This PR implements a layered approach: 1) a lightweight PII classifier + heuristic regex scrubber exposed as internal/handoff/scrubber.go with three redact levels (none, redact-pii, redact-sensitive) and config driven allowlists; 2) an attachment proxy service internal/proxy/attach_proxy.go that accepts multipart uploads, applies size/type heuristics, stores content in an ephemeral blobstore with TTL and returns short access tokens; 3) enforcement of per-incident and per-user upload rate limits via a sliding-window counter stored in Redis with configurable quotas and backoff; 4) telemetry and audit hooks i...

title: add incident-capture CLI plus ephemeral playbook templates and safe artifact bundler
preview: Motivation: oncall responders repeatedly need a compact, reproducible snapshot of system state that is safe to share with SRE/eng. Existing runbook generators either produce too much PII or require interactive console steps. This PR introduces a lightweight CLI that: 1) collects a tunable set of diagnostics (logs, s...
text:
Motivation: oncall responders repeatedly need a compact, reproducible snapshot of system state that is safe to share with SRE/eng. Existing runbook generators either produce too much PII or require interactive console steps. This PR introduces a lightweight CLI that: 1) collects a tunable set of diagnostics (logs, system metrics, trace snippets, recent error samples), 2) performs automated redaction rules and entropy checks to avoid shipping secrets, and 3) packages artifacts into an ephemeral bundle with a reproducible hash and a short-lived presigned link for sharing. In addition, we include a small library of ephemeral playbook templates (network-isolation, safe-restart, rolling-back) that are parameterized and renderable by the incident-bot UI.

What changed (high level): added new binary `cmd/incident-capture` with subcommands `collect`, `redact`, `bundle`, `preview`; new Go pack...

title: Post error budget burn summaries to #eng-sre on burn-rate alerts (dashboards + runbook links)
preview: Context: As part of the tiered SLO + error budget program, we’re standardizing burn-rate alerting and enforcement. Today the burn-rate alerts page the on-call, but triage is still slow because the initial signal doesn’t include (a) how fast we’re burning, (b) what’s driving it (latency vs errors vs streaming disconn...
text:
Context: As part of the tiered SLO + error budget program, we’re standardizing burn-rate alerting and enforcement. Today the burn-rate alerts page the on-call, but triage is still slow because the initial signal doesn’t include (a) how fast we’re burning, (b) what’s driving it (latency vs errors vs streaming disconnects), or (c) where to go next (dashboards/runbooks). This PR updates incident-bot to listen for burn-rate alert notifications and post a structured “burn summary” message into #eng-sre (and thread replies for updates), including deep links to the tiered SLO dashboards and the active mitigation runbook.

What this does: 1) Adds a new alert type handler for error budget burn-rate alerts (Prometheus Alertmanager payloads via our existing webhook). 2) Builds a canonical Slack message format: header with SLO name + tier + region, a short diagnosis (burn rate + window + budget r...

title: Sessioned runbook orchestrator and alert seatbelt
preview: Context and motivation: On-call teams repeatedly asked for a way to bind ephemeral debugging sessions to runbooks so that responders and secondary engineers can join a single context (links, logs, temporary access) and avoid duplicate work. In parallel we needed a short-term noise-dampening mechanism (an "alert seat...
text: Context and motivation: On-call teams repeatedly asked for a way to bind ephemeral debugging sessions to runbooks so that responders and secondary engineers can join a single context (links, logs, temporary access) and avoid duplicate work. In parallel we needed a short-term noise-dampening mechanism (an "alert seatbelt") that automatically tempers noisy symptom alerts while keeping SLO-critical signals unsuppressed. This PR implements a sessioned runbook orchestrator plus an SLA-aware alert seatbelt feature.

title: Prompt for + pin related SUP tickets and customer-impact summary in incident bridge channels
preview: Context: Support/SRE asked for a consistent way to keep customer escalations discoverable after a bridge ends. Today, bridge channels frequently miss links to the SUP ticket(s), and the customer-impact summary gets repeated across Slack/Jira/email with drift. This PR updates incident-bot to (1) prompt the bridge cre...
text: Context: Support/SRE asked for a consistent way to keep customer escalations discoverable after a bridge ends. Today, bridge channels frequently miss links to the SUP ticket(s), and the customer-impact summary gets repeated across Slack/Jira/email with drift. This PR updates incident-bot to (1) prompt the bridge creator for related SUP tickets and a customer-impact summary, (2) post and pin a single "Escalation context" message in the bridge channel, and (3) keep the channel topic synced with SUP links (without including customer names). Behavior: - When a bridge channel is created via the bot (or when the bot detects it has been invited to a newly created bridge channel), the bot posts a message with two required actions: "Add related Jira tickets" (accepts SUP-* and optionally INT-*), and "Add customer impact" (free text w/ guidance). - Once submitted, the bot edits the message into...

title: Enforce owner/type (and postmortem link for Sev0/Sev1) before incident close
preview: Context: As part of the Reliability Program (incident taxonomy + ownership mapping), we need incident records to be consistently classifiable and auditable. Today, incidents can be closed without an owner, without incident_type/subtype, and (for high severity) without any postmortem placeholder. This makes reporting...
text:
Context: As part of the Reliability Program (incident taxonomy + ownership mapping), we need incident records to be consistently classifiable and auditable. Today, incidents can be closed without an owner, without incident_type/subtype, and (for high severity) without any postmortem placeholder. This makes reporting incomplete and creates churn for SRE/Support when trying to backfill or follow up after the fact.

What this PR does: 1) Adds closure gating to the /incident close flow (Slack modal + command path). An incident cannot be marked Resolved/Closed unless incident_type and subtype are set (or explicitly set to unknown/other per policy) and owner is present (resolved owner via mapping or manually set owner). 2) For Sev0 and Sev1 only, requires a postmortem link OR a placeholder follow-up ticket link (Linear/Jira) before close. The UI copy is explicit: “postmortem doc link or pla...

title: shifted telemetry compressor and humanized handoff note generator
preview: Motivation: On-call handoffs often arrive with large telemetry attachments and ambiguous next steps. This PR introduces a compact telemetry compressor and a humanized handoff note generator to reduce payload size, remove PII, and provide a cognitive-load estimate and a canary-safe rollback playbook seeded from the c...
text: Motivation: On-call handoffs often arrive with large telemetry attachments and ambiguous next steps. This PR introduces a compact telemetry compressor and a humanized handoff note generator to reduce payload size, remove PII, and provide a cognitive-load estimate and a canary-safe rollback playbook seeded from the collected snapshot. The snapshot collector produces a small 'repro trailer' containing minimal trace slices, top-k logs by error signature, sampled heap/disk metadata and a KV diff bundle. The compressor uses a delta-based rolling window (inspired by rsync-style chunking) plus optional gzip fallback to guarantee size and decompression determinism across platforms. The handoff note generator templates the critical actions, suggested playbook steps, and a short 'operator confidence' metric based on recent similar incidents and successful mitigations. Implementation: adds a new...

title: Add /rollback shortcut command to post rollback UI + runbook links in incident channels
preview:
Title: Add /rollback shortcut command to post rollback UI + runbook links in incident channels

Context
During recent gamedays + the SUP-18421 escalation, the biggest time sink was not executing rollback itself, but getting responders to the right place quickly (Console rollback view + the canonical runbook) while c...
text:
Title: Add /rollback shortcut command to post rollback UI + runbook links in incident channels

Context
During recent gamedays + the SUP-18421 escalation, the biggest time sink was not executing rollback itself, but getting responders to the right place quickly (Console rollback view + the canonical runbook) while context switching across tabs. This PR adds a tiny “fast path” in incident-bot: a slash command that posts the correct links and a short checklist directly into the incident channel.

What’s included
- New Slack slash command: `/rollback` (alias `/rw-rollback`).
- Posts a message with:
 - Link to Console Rollback UI (Optimization Config history view with the rollback modal entry point).
 - Link to the runbook: “Rollback Optimization Config”.
 - A reminder of the expected decision criteria (p95/p99 regression + 5xx) and the post-rollback verification checklist.
- Optional arg...

title: Auto-suggest incident_type from symptoms (5xx/latency/streaming/capacity) with override
preview: Context: As part of the Reliability Program "Incident taxonomy + ownership mapping" we’re making incident_type/subtype required in incident-bot. This PR adds lightweight heuristics to auto-suggest a likely incident type based on the initial symptoms so we reduce time-to-classify during live incidents while keeping t...
text:
Context: As part of the Reliability Program "Incident taxonomy + ownership mapping" we’re making incident_type/subtype required in incident-bot. This PR adds lightweight heuristics to auto-suggest a likely incident type based on the initial symptoms so we reduce time-to-classify during live incidents while keeping the human as the source of truth.

What’s in this PR: 1) Suggestion engine: Adds a new suggestion module that generates a (type, subtype, confidence, rationale) suggestion from the incident creation context. Inputs include: incident-bot symptom selections (5xx spike, p95 latency regression, streaming disconnects/resets, capacity/fleet alerts), any attached alert payload (title + common labels), and a small set of structured hints we already capture (tier, region, affected surface: hosted/dedicated/private). The output is always constrained to the v1 taxonomy allowlist; if we...

title: Post overload-protection activations to #incidents with dashboards + runbook links
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds a new incident-bot event handler for Hot-route Capacity Protection activation events emitted by routing/gateway. When protection enters an active state (or exits), incident-bot posts a structured Slack message to `#incidents` with:
- route_group + route (when available), region, tier class, a...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds a new incident-bot event handler for Hot-route Capacity Protection activation events emitted by routing/gateway. When protection enters an active state (or exits), incident-bot posts a structured Slack message to `#incidents` with:
- route_group + route (when available), region, tier class, and activation type
- primary reason code(s) (quota vs overload) and protection mode (admission control vs shedding)
- current/peak metrics snapshot (shed_rate, 429_rate, p95 latency, queue depth where present)
- links to the SRE dashboard + runbook section
- a dedupe key to avoid paging storms during flappy thresholds

This is intended to reduce time-to-diagnosis during overload events and make it obvious when best-effort is being throttled to preserve enterprise SLOs.

## Context
Hot-route capacity protection is rolling out behind flags (see ENG-4927). During the us-east c...

title: Require incident_type/subtype on create; auto-resolve owner + escalation channel
preview:
Context: As part of the Reliability Program “Incident taxonomy + ownership mapping”, we’re making incident_type a required field at incident creation time, and we want the bot to resolve an owner team consistently (single primary owner) to reduce time-to-engage and improve reporting quality.

This PR updates the Sla...
text:
Context: As part of the Reliability Program “Incident taxonomy + ownership mapping”, we’re making incident_type a required field at incident creation time, and we want the bot to resolve an owner team consistently (single primary owner) to reduce time-to-engage and improve reporting quality.

This PR updates the Slack modal + slash command flow to require incident_type and incident_subtype (from the v1 allowlist), then performs owner resolution (type/subtype -> primary owner + escalation channel) and posts the result into the incident channel. The goal is: (1) classification is required and auditable, (2) owner is consistently derived from a versioned mapping, and (3) the IC gets an immediate “who owns this” + “where to escalate” suggestion.

User-facing changes:
- /incident create now blocks submission until incident_type is selected.
- incident_subtype is required when the type has ...

title: Add perf-canary alert handler (Slack threads, dedupe, runbook links)
preview:
Summary: Adds a first-class handler in incident-bot for perf-canary regression alerts. The handler formats alerts into a consistent Slack thread, de-dupes repeated firings, and attaches deep links (Grafana dashboard + Confluence runbook + relevant traces) to speed triage.

Context: The perf-canary service will emit ...
text:
Summary: Adds a first-class handler in incident-bot for perf-canary regression alerts. The handler formats alerts into a consistent Slack thread, de-dupes repeated firings, and attaches deep links (Grafana dashboard + Confluence runbook + relevant traces) to speed triage.

Context: The perf-canary service will emit alerts as a webhook payload (model + region + tier + metric + observed vs baseline + confidence + baseline version/runtime tags). Before this PR, incident-bot treated canary payloads as generic webhooks which led to noisy, non-threaded posts and no reliable dedupe key, making it hard to triage during regressions and especially during baseline drift/threshold tuning.

What this PR does: 1) Adds a new webhook route + handler for source=perf-canary (Content-Type: application/json). 2) Builds a stable dedupe key and thread key so repeat evaluations (same model/metric/region/sev...

title: Support approval step for Sev0/Sev1 status page publishes (optional)
preview: Context: As part of the Status page transparency upgrades initiative, we’ve standardized comms templates and added Comms Lead reminders. One gap that came up repeatedly (Support escalations + game days) is that during Sev0/Sev1 incidents we sometimes publish status updates with wording that Support has to immediatel...
text:
Context: As part of the Status page transparency upgrades initiative, we’ve standardized comms templates and added Comms Lead reminders. One gap that came up repeatedly (Support escalations + game days) is that during Sev0/Sev1 incidents we sometimes publish status updates with wording that Support has to immediately clarify, or we publish a component impact that later turns out to be wrong (especially when impact is limited to a subset of customers/regions). This PR introduces an optional approval step in incident-bot before publishing status page updates for Sev0/Sev1, allowing either (a) the on-call Incident Commander or (b) an on-call Support lead to approve the draft. This is intended to improve accuracy without adding overhead to Sev2+ events.

What’s changing:
- Adds a configurable “publish requires approval” gate that activates only for Sev0/Sev1 incidents and only when the ac...

title: Statuspage API client: retry/backoff + idempotency keys + audit log entries
preview: Problem: incident-bot’s Statuspage integration currently performs best-effort POST/PATCH calls with minimal retry handling. During Sev0/Sev1s we’ve seen transient 5xx/timeout/429 responses (plus occasional network blips) leading to (a) failed publishes that require manual retry and (b) duplicate updates when a human...
text: Problem: incident-bot’s Statuspage integration currently performs best-effort POST/PATCH calls with minimal retry handling. During Sev0/Sev1s we’ve seen transient 5xx/timeout/429 responses (plus occasional network blips) leading to (a) failed publishes that require manual retry and (b) duplicate updates when a human re-triggers a step while the original request is still in flight. This PR introduces a small Statuspage API client wrapper with standardized retry/backoff, idempotency keys, and structured audit log entries for every attempted publish/update. Scope: add an HTTP client with (1) bounded retries for retryable failures (429, 5xx, network timeouts), (2) exponential backoff with jitter and respect for Retry-After where present, (3) deterministic idempotency keys so retries and replays don’t create duplicate incidents/updates, and (4) audit logging to our internal incident-bot au...

title: Generate Statuspage update drafts from incident state (phase templates + metrics)
preview: Context: As part of the status page transparency upgrades, we want to reduce time-to-first-update and improve consistency across incident phases without increasing on-call burden. This PR adds a draft generator that produces templated Statuspage update text based on the current incident state (Investigating/Identifi...
text:
Context: As part of the status page transparency upgrades, we want to reduce time-to-first-update and improve consistency across incident phases without increasing on-call burden. This PR adds a draft generator that produces templated Statuspage update text based on the current incident state (Investigating/Identified/Mitigating/Monitoring/Resolved). Drafts pull in the structured fields we already collect at intake (component/region/capability) plus optional impact metrics and known mitigations. The output is a DRAFT only (explicitly labeled) and requires a human publish step.

What this does: Adds a template registry for each incident phase with a small variable set (component_display, regions, capabilities, started_at, current_state, impact_summary, error_rate, p95_latency_ms, affected_routes, customer_visible_symptoms, mitigations_in_progress, fallback_active, next_update_eta). Add...

title: Announce circuit breaker state transitions to #incidents and #eng-oncall
preview:
Context
As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding program (ENG-4821), we need a low-latency, human-visible signal when breakers start shedding (OPEN) and when they recover (HALF_OPEN/CLOSED). The current workflow relies on dashboards/alerts only; during incidents this increases time-to-diagnosis and con...
text:
Context
As part of the Hosted circuit breakers + load shedding program (ENG-4821), we need a low-latency, human-visible signal when breakers start shedding (OPEN) and when they recover (HALF_OPEN/CLOSED). The current workflow relies on dashboards/alerts only; during incidents this increases time-to-diagnosis and contributes to duplicate triage.

This PR teaches incident-bot to announce breaker state transitions into Slack (#incidents and #eng-oncall) with:
- Transition summary (CLOSED → OPEN, OPEN → HALF_OPEN, HALF_OPEN → CLOSED)
- Scope (region, route, model selector, tenant/tier when available)
- Top contributing signal(s) (queue depth, token/sec saturation, dependency health)
- Links to dashboards and the oncall runbook
- A short “recommended actions” block aligned with the runbook

Behavior
- Posts OPEN transitions to both #incidents and #eng-oncall.
- Posts recovery transitions (...

title: Incident intake: add required status page component/region/capability fields + validation
preview: Why: We keep losing time (and occasionally accuracy) in the first 10–15 minutes of an incident because the intake form doesn’t capture the info required to pick the right public status page component(s). This led to coarse updates ("API") even when the impact was limited to Streaming or Embeddings in a single region...
text:
Why: We keep losing time (and occasionally accuracy) in the first 10–15 minutes of an incident because the intake form doesn’t capture the info required to pick the right public status page component(s). This led to coarse updates ("API") even when the impact was limited to Streaming or Embeddings in a single region. This PR updates the incident intake workflow to require three customer-facing classification fields (component, region, capability) and adds validation + reminders so Comms Lead can publish component-accurate updates quickly.

What changed (high level): - Adds new required intake fields: status_component_id, impacted_region, impacted_capability - Adds opinionated defaults + "Unknown/TBD" escape hatch to avoid blocking declaration - Adds server-side validation for required fields on incident creation + updates - Writes selected values into the incident record (used later b...

title: Add Comms Lead role assignment + TTFU reminders; log status updates to #incidents
preview: Context: We’ve had repeated incidents where we miss the first customer-facing update window (TTFU), and ownership of incident communications is ambiguous when the IC is deep in mitigation. This PR adds an explicit Comms Lead role to the incident-bot workflow, adds configurable reminders tied to severity (TTFU target...
text: Context: We’ve had repeated incidents where we miss the first customer-facing update window (TTFU), and ownership of incident communications is ambiguous when the IC is deep in mitigation. This PR adds an explicit Comms Lead role to the incident-bot workflow, adds configurable reminders tied to severity (TTFU targets), and logs customer-facing update actions to #incidents for shared situational awareness and later audits. Changes: (1) Incident intake now captures/assigns a Comms Lead (default: secondary responder if present; else falls back to IC; can be reassigned). (2) Bot posts a lightweight “TTFU reminder” in the incident Slack channel if no status update has been logged within the target window (Sev0/Sev1 only by default). (3) Any status update action initiated via bot commands is echoed to #incidents with incident link + component(s) + state (Investigating/Identified/etc.) and t...

title: Ephemeral runbook cache and idempotent rollforward handler for safe post-incident triage
preview: Context and motivation: On high-noise incidents the incident-bot runbook lookups and remediation orchestration were adding 150-300ms per request and occasionally executing non-idempotent handlers twice when a race in the triage state machine retried a step. This PR introduces a bounded ephemeral runbook cache (in-me...
text: Context and motivation: On high-noise incidents the incident-bot runbook lookups and remediation orchestration were adding 150-300ms per request and occasionally executing non-idempotent handlers twice when a race in the triage state machine retried a step. This PR introduces a bounded ephemeral runbook cache (in-memory with TTL and stale-while-revalidate) to reduce lookup latency, and a 'rollforward' handler wrapper that enforces idempotency and safe retries for automated remediation actions. Goals: 1) reduce median oncall lookup latency under load, 2) prevent double-applies of non-idempotent remediation steps, 3) preserve existing runbook semantics and allow opt-out via annotation. Changes summary: - runbook cache: lightweight LRU with 1s default TTL, 5s stale-while-revalidate, metrics and per-team tunables - rollforward handler: decorator that records ephemeral execution tokens in ...

title: Ephemeral playbook preview + safe-autoreply gateway for low-severity alerts
preview: Motivation: On-call responders spend time drafting and validating small mitigation steps for noisy, low-severity alerts (timeouts, retries, downstream 4xx spikes). This PR introduces two related features to reduce cognitive load and MTTD: (1) an ephemeral playbook preview UI/CLI that generates a short, editable sequ...
text:
Motivation: On-call responders spend time drafting and validating small mitigation steps for noisy, low-severity alerts (timeouts, retries, downstream 4xx spikes). This PR introduces two related features to reduce cognitive load and MTTD: (1) an ephemeral playbook preview UI/CLI that generates a short, editable sequence of suggested remediation steps from the incident context and recent telemetry; (2) a Safe Autoreply Gateway that can optionally send gated automated replies to ticketing/chat channels for low-risk alerts (acknowledgement + recommended self-serve steps), with rate-limiting, audit trails, and kill-switches.

What it does: - Ephemeral playbook preview: incidentctl preview-playbook <alert-id> produces a compact, human-readable playbook draft (3-5 steps) derived from the runbook templates, recent traces, and cached diagnostics. Previews are ephemeral (TTL 24h) and stored en...

title: Add incident template/playbook for Provision-to-Ready (GPU provisioning) failures
preview:
## Summary
Adds a dedicated incident template + lightweight playbook for Provision-to-Ready / Dedicated fleet bring-up failures (nodes stuck in Provisioning/Validating, mass quarantine, image/driver mismatch, join failures, NCCL smoke flakes).

This is intended to standardize the minimum info we capture in #incident...
text:
## Summary
Adds a dedicated incident template + lightweight playbook for Provision-to-Ready / Dedicated fleet bring-up failures (nodes stuck in Provisioning/Validating, mass quarantine, image/driver mismatch, join failures, NCCL smoke flakes).

This is intended to standardize the minimum info we capture in #incidents and in the incident doc so SRE/Infra/Platform can triage quickly and we can compute time-to-ready and failure-rate impacts without back-and-forth.

## Why
During the staged rollout we saw a few common patterns:
- Missing pool metadata (region/GPU type/image channel), which slowed down correlating failures to a specific rollout.
- Unclear ownership handoff (Infra vs Platform vs SRE) depending on whether the failure is “can’t join cluster” vs “validation gate failing” vs “readiness service/API regressions”.
- Links to dashboards/runbooks living in different places and being...

title: Annotate upgrade-window incidents + link to Private release artifacts
preview:
## Summary
When a Private upgrade is running (VPC or on-prem), we want incidents that fire in that window to be clearly labeled and automatically correlated to the exact release artifacts involved.

This PR updates incident-bot’s enrichment pipeline to:
- detect upgrade windows based on a canonical source (release t...
text:
## Summary
When a Private upgrade is running (VPC or on-prem), we want incidents that fire in that window to be clearly labeled and automatically correlated to the exact release artifacts involved.

This PR updates incident-bot’s enrichment pipeline to:
- detect upgrade windows based on a canonical source (release train + installer upgrade state signals)
- tag incidents created during those windows as upgrade-related
- attach links to relevant release artifacts (release tag, build provenance, and installer plan bundle) so on-call can quickly determine whether a spike is likely upgrade-induced vs. product regression.

This is part of the "Private upgrade + rollback mechanism" workstream focused on auditability and reduced MTTR.

## Motivation
We’ve repeatedly had to manually correlate incidents during maintenance windows:
- Was there an upgrade running?
- Which Private version / chart ...

title: Fix timeout handling in telemetry queries (retries/backoff + better degraded-mode messaging)
preview: Context: During incidents we often burst telemetry queries (GPU util, queue depth, KV cache pressure, scheduler backpressure). When telemetry is slow or partially degraded, the bot currently either (a) times out and shows empty cards, or (b) holds the Slack interaction long enough that users think the bot is broken....
text:
Context: During incidents we often burst telemetry queries (GPU util, queue depth, KV cache pressure, scheduler backpressure). When telemetry is slow or partially degraded, the bot currently either (a) times out and shows empty cards, or (b) holds the Slack interaction long enough that users think the bot is broken. This came up in INT-3341 and in recent #eng-infra / #eng-sre threads about bounding diagnostics load.

What this PR changes: 1) Adds a hard end-to-end deadline for telemetry collection per card render (default 4.5s, configurable), with per-query timeouts and cancellation propagation so we don’t keep hammering telemetry after the user path has moved on. 2) Implements bounded retries with exponential backoff + jitter for retryable classes (timeouts, 429, 502/503/504). Retries are capped (max 2) and respect the overall deadline. 3) Improves degraded-mode messaging in Slack ca...

title: Feature flag + cohort targeting for Incident Bot v2 diagnostics output
preview: Summary: Introduces a gated rollout mechanism for Incident Bot v2 diagnostics (deploy context/config diffs/saturation signals) so we can run a limited beta (SRE rotation + selected regions/channels) and safely roll back to v1 output if bot latency or upstream query cost spikes during incidents. Motivation: v2 diagno...
text: Summary: Introduces a gated rollout mechanism for Incident Bot v2 diagnostics (deploy context/config diffs/saturation signals) so we can run a limited beta (SRE rotation + selected regions/channels) and safely roll back to v1 output if bot latency or upstream query cost spikes during incidents. Motivation: v2 diagnostics increases upstream call volume and payload size; we need controlled exposure + a kill switch to avoid self-inflicted load during Sev events. Implementation details: Adds a new feature-flag abstraction (DiagV2Gate) with cohort targeting on Slack context (workspace, channel, user group), incident context (env/region/service), and optional allow/deny lists. Default behavior is OFF unless explicitly enabled; bot continues to render v1 cards with deep links. Adds three evaluation modes: (1) global enable/disable (INCIDENT_BOT_DIAG_V2_ENABLED), (2) cohort allowlist (INCIDEN...

title: Slack UX: modal pagination for large context + per-thread caching for repeated interactions
preview: Problem: large config diffs and saturation signal payloads frequently exceed what fits cleanly in a single Slack message block set. In practice responders were seeing (a) aggressive truncation that hid the one line they needed, and (b) repeated clicks/refreshes in a thread that re-ran expensive upstream queries, whi...
text:
Problem: large config diffs and saturation signal payloads frequently exceed what fits cleanly in a single Slack message block set. In practice responders were seeing (a) aggressive truncation that hid the one line they needed, and (b) repeated clicks/refreshes in a thread that re-ran expensive upstream queries, which is the opposite of what we want during an incident.

This PR introduces two changes for incident-bot v2 diagnostics rendering:

1) Modal pagination for large content (diffs + signal tables)
- Adds a "View details" button on cards that opens a Slack modal.
- Modal supports pagination (prev/next) with page size tuned for Slack's block/text limits.
- Supports both "unified" diff rendering and "key/value change list" rendering (whichever the card produced), using the same pagination component.
- Retains the existing short preview in the thread for quick scanning.

2) Per-thr...

title: prioritized-rca-scoring-and-playbook-recommender
preview:
Motivation: on-call responders are overwhelmed by alert noise and often run a suite of irrelevant playbooks. This PR implements a lightweight root-cause scoring layer + playbook prioritizer that surfaces the most likely mitigations.

What it does (single-pass summary):
- Adds a signal fusion module that scores candi...
text:
Motivation: on-call responders are overwhelmed by alert noise and often run a suite of irrelevant playbooks. This PR implements a lightweight root-cause scoring layer + playbook prioritizer that surfaces the most likely mitigations.

What it does (single-pass summary):
- Adds a signal fusion module that scores candidate root causes from alert metadata, recent SLO burn-rate windows (via slo-toolkit hooks), and recent deploy/rollout activity.
- Introduces a playbook prioritizer that ranks runbooks by expected mitigation probability and time-to-mitigate (TTM).
- Adds a new /incident suggest-playbook command for Slack that returns 1-3 prioritized actions and confidence bands.
- Exposes an opt-in per-service configuration to tune weights and to pin preferred playbooks.
- Includes metrics and tracing for the scoring decisions to aid audits and regression debugging.

Why this is different fr...

title: deferred playbook executor with scheduled dry-run and safe rollback hooks
preview: Motivation: On-call automation needs a way to schedule runbooks to execute outside peak windows, run a non-destructive dry-run before live execution, and ensure safe automatic rollback when automation makes state changes. This PR adds a Deferred Playbook Executor that accepts scheduled windows and optional dry-run p...
text: Motivation: On-call automation needs a way to schedule runbooks to execute outside peak windows, run a non-destructive dry-run before live execution, and ensure safe automatic rollback when automation makes state changes. This PR adds a Deferred Playbook Executor that accepts scheduled windows and optional dry-run payloads, a scheduled dry-run runner that simulates playbook steps without side effects, and pluggable safe-rollback hooks that capture pre-change snapshots and provide an automated revert path. Key design points: (1) All deferred executions are stored as immutable scheduling tasks with idempotency tokens, (2) dry-run mode routes actions to a simulator layer that returns predicted outcomes and a list of mutated resources, (3) rollback hooks register resource snapshotters and revert actions, and (4) RBAC/feature-flag gating prevents accidental live scheduling. This change als...

title: Add saturation signal queries (GPU/queue/KV cache) to incident context payload
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds a new “Saturation signals” card to the incident-bot v2 context payload and Slack rendering. The card pulls a bounded set of telemetry signals for the affected service/region over a short window (default: last 15m) and summarizes them using simple thresholding so responders can quickly decide ...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds a new “Saturation signals” card to the incident-bot v2 context payload and Slack rendering. The card pulls a bounded set of telemetry signals for the affected service/region over a short window (default: last 15m) and summarizes them using simple thresholding so responders can quickly decide whether they’re dealing with capacity/scheduler pressure vs. an application/deploy regression.

Signals included (initial set): (1) GPU utilization (avg/p95), (2) scheduler/ingress queue depth (p95 + max), (3) KV cache pressure (p95), plus optional backpressure/admission-control indicators when present.

This is designed to be safe under incident conditions: strict timeouts, query budget enforcement, and an explicit degraded-mode response (cached snapshot + dashboard links) when live queries fail.

## Motivation
Multiple recent incidents (e.g., EU-west capacity saturation +...

title: Redact secrets in config diffs before rendering to Slack
preview: Problem: Incident-bot v2 is starting to surface tenant-safe config diffs in #incidents and #eng-oncall, but the current formatting is purely syntactic and can leak secrets if upstream config contains inline credentials/tokens (or if a value looks like a secret). This PR adds a deterministic redaction layer that runs...
text: Problem: Incident-bot v2 is starting to surface tenant-safe config diffs in #incidents and #eng-oncall, but the current formatting is purely syntactic and can leak secrets if upstream config contains inline credentials/tokens (or if a value looks like a secret). This PR adds a deterministic redaction layer that runs before any diff content is rendered to Slack (cards, modals, and copy-to-clipboard). Approach: (1) Add key-based redaction (denylist of sensitive key names + path-based matches). (2) Add value-based secret detection (JWTs, API keys, common token prefixes, PEM blocks, base64-ish long strings, AWS-like access keys, etc.) with conservative thresholds. (3) Add allowlisted keys/paths that are safe to show even if they trip generic heuristics (e.g., numeric limits, routing weights, non-sensitive feature flag booleans). (4) Replace redacted values with stable placeholders ("[REDA...

title: Add deploy context card (recent deploys + canary status) to incident thread diagnostics
preview: Motivation: During recent incidents we repeatedly lost time answering “what changed?” in the first 5 minutes. This PR adds a new Incident Bot diagnostics card that attaches deploy context directly into the incident thread, including recent deploys for the affected service/region, rollout phase, canary health, and co...
text: Motivation: During recent incidents we repeatedly lost time answering “what changed?” in the first 5 minutes. This PR adds a new Incident Bot diagnostics card that attaches deploy context directly into the incident thread, including recent deploys for the affected service/region, rollout phase, canary health, and commit/image identifiers. It’s designed to be tenant-safe and useful even when deeper telemetry is timing out. Scope: (1) New Slack “Recent deploys” context card rendered in the thread reply alongside existing diagnostics cards. (2) Backend integration to fetch recent deploy metadata from the release registry (service -> last N deploy events) and normalize it into the bot’s context payload. (3) Links out to the deploy timeline view and the referenced commit/PR when available. UX notes: The card summarizes up to 3 most recent deploys in the last 6 hours, then provides a “View ...

title: Add config diff collector (config-service integration) + Slack formatting
preview:
Summary
This PR wires Incident Bot v2 into the new config-service diff endpoint and adds a first-pass Slack-safe rendering for config diffs. The goal is to surface tenant-safe config deltas (feature flags, routing policy, quotas/rate limits, model pins) in the first 5 minutes of an incident without responders needin...
text:
Summary
This PR wires Incident Bot v2 into the new config-service diff endpoint and adds a first-pass Slack-safe rendering for config diffs. The goal is to surface tenant-safe config deltas (feature flags, routing policy, quotas/rate limits, model pins) in the first 5 minutes of an incident without responders needing to jump through manual queries.

Scope
- Adds a ConfigDiffCollector that calls config-service (internal) to fetch a bounded diff for a (tenant, service, region, time window).
- Adds basic formatting for Slack: compact summary card + optional “expand” view payload (used by later Slack modal work).
- Adds guardrails: query timeouts, size limits, allowlisted keys (with explicit “redacted/omitted” markers), and stable ordering to reduce diff noise.
- Adds audit context headers (requestor + incident thread id) so config-service can emit audit events (event emission handled in ...

title: Introduce adaptive pager suppression using noise profiling
preview: Motivation: paging fatigue from repetitive low-value alerts continues to erode oncall bandwidth. This change adds an adaptive pager-suppression subsystem to incident-bot that builds and uses per-service noise profiles to decide when to suppress, group, or throttle notifications. Key goals: reduce noisy low-action pa...
text:
Motivation: paging fatigue from repetitive low-value alerts continues to erode oncall bandwidth. This change adds an adaptive pager-suppression subsystem to incident-bot that builds and uses per-service noise profiles to decide when to suppress, group, or throttle notifications. Key goals: reduce noisy low-action pages by ~40% in a test cohort, provide clear feedback to oncall (why a page was suppressed), and make suppression policies configurable and auditable.

What this PR does: 
- Adds a lightweight noise profiler that accumulates sketch summaries of alert fingerprints (hash, signature dimensions) and computes short-window noise scores (noise_rate, dedupe_entropy, throttle_suggestion).
- Implements an adaptive suppression policy engine that can: suppress (no page), group (single consolidated page for an incident burst), or debounce (delay page by Xs) based on policy rules and the ...
